content.claim: Hungary adopted free school-choice systems for elementary schools with discretionary pupil selection, creating segregation by school characteristics before formal age-12 gimnázium tracking begins, suggesting structural change to 6/8-year tracks alone may not resolve segregation originating in primary-school choice
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: School-choice effects are documented correlatively, not causally isolated from tracking effects
Unclear whether primary-school choice segregation would persist or decline if secondary tracking were delayed without concurrent choice-system reform
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
